company_overview: Meridian Bank is a mid-sized Brazilian financial institution with R$180B in assets under management, 4.2 million retail customers, and 850,000 SME clients across 12 states. The bank operates a hybrid branch-digital model and is under competitive pressure from digital-native fintechs. Meridian has a dedicated data science team of 40 analysts and an active cloud migration program (AWS-first) initiated in 2024.
business_challenges: KYC and AML compliance review consumes 1,200 analyst hours per month, with a 14-day average SLA that creates friction in account opening
Call center handles 2.1 million contacts per month with 38% first-call resolution — significantly below the 62% industry benchmark
Credit risk models rely on 18-month-old training data, limiting responsiveness to macroeconomic shifts
Regulatory reporting for BACEN takes 3 weeks per quarter and involves manual extraction from 7 different core systems
WhatsApp channel (used by 1.8M customers) currently offers only rule-based FAQ responses with no contextual understanding

ai_opportunities.business_impact: Reduce KYC review time from 14 days to under 2 days. Save 900+ analyst hours/month. Estimated annual savings: R$4.2M
Deflect 50-60% of tier-1 call center contacts. Improve NPS by 15-20 points. Estimated savings: R$8.1M/year in call center operations
Reduce quarterly reporting effort from 3 weeks to 3 days. Eliminate manual extraction errors. Estimated compliance risk reduction: R$2M/year
Reduce non-performing loan rate by 1.2-1.8 percentage points. Estimated impact on credit losses: R$12M/year reduction
Increase SME product attachment rate by 18%. Estimated revenue uplift: R$5.6M/year

key_stakeholders.title: Chief Digital Officer
key_stakeholders.likely_priorities: Accelerating digital transformation KPIs, reducing time-to-market for new features, demonstrating AI ROI to the board
key_stakeholders.engagement_tip: Lead with business outcomes and timeline to value. Reference comparable Brazilian bank deployments. Avoid deep technical architecture in initial meetings.

key_stakeholders.title: Chief Risk Officer
key_stakeholders.likely_priorities: BACEN compliance, model explainability, data sovereignty (Brazil data residency), audit trail for AI decisions
key_stakeholders.engagement_tip: Address data residency proactively. Bring compliance documentation. Emphasize full audit logging and human-in-the-loop escalation paths.

key_stakeholders.title: Head of Retail Banking
key_stakeholders.likely_priorities: Customer experience scores (NPS, CSAT), call center efficiency, digital engagement rates
key_stakeholders.engagement_tip: Focus on the WhatsApp and call center use cases. Show before/after customer journey maps. Quantify deflection rates with benchmark data.

key_stakeholders.title: VP of Technology / CTO
key_stakeholders.likely_priorities: AWS architecture alignment, API-first integration, scalability, vendor lock-in risk
key_stakeholders.engagement_tip: Engage on architecture early. Demonstrate API-first approach, SDK availability, and how the solution integrates with existing AWS infrastructure.

recommended_opening_questions: What's your current KYC SLA target vs. actual, and what's the business impact of missing it?
How many WhatsApp contacts per month go unanswered or require human escalation today?
What's your data science team's current capacity — are they resourced to manage a new AI system?
Where does AI sit on your board agenda for 2026 — is there a specific mandate or budget already allocated?
What's your current BACEN reporting process, and where does the most manual effort occur?
competitive_landscape: Bradesco and Itaú have both announced significant AI investments (R$2B+ combined) focused on customer service and credit. Nubank is raising the bar on AI-native UX. Meridian risks losing SME and younger retail customers to both incumbents with deeper pockets and fintechs with superior digital experience.
recommended_solution_approach: Start with a focused POC on the WhatsApp Virtual Assistant — high ROI, visible to end customers, and achievable in 12-16 weeks without requiring deep core banking integration. Use this as a reference win to unlock budget for the higher-complexity KYC and regulatory reporting use cases.
risks_and_objections: Data sovereignty: Meridian will require Brazil data residency — address with Azure Brazil South deployment option
BACEN auditability: AI decisions in credit and compliance must be fully explainable — prepare model cards and audit trail documentation
Integration complexity: Core banking systems are on-premise legacy — plan API gateway layer as integration pattern
Internal AI skepticism: Some business units may resist AI-driven process changes — recommend change management workstream alongside technical delivery
Budget cycle: If budget not allocated, a no-cost POC scoped to WhatsApp can demonstrate value before full investment decision
